name: Customizing the Chart | Lightweight Charts
snippet: This tutorial provides an introduction to customizing Lightweight Chart's appearance and functionality.

name: tradingview/lightweight-charts: Performant financial charts built with HTML5 canvas
snippet: TradingView Lightweight Charts are one of the smallest and fastest financial HTML5 charts. The Lightweight Charts library is the best choice for you

name: Custom indicators | Advanced Charts Documentation
snippet:
Advanced Charts and Trading Platform support more than 100 indicators. You can also create your custom indicators in JavaScript using the library's API. The ...
Advanced Charts and Trading Platform support more than 100 indicators. You can also create your custom indicators in JavaScript using the library's API. The API allows you to create a variety of indicators using different plot types, styles, colors, and mathematical functions.

name: Is is possible to add a study/indicator to the chart? · tradingview lightweight-charts · Discussion #647
snippet:
Dec 11, 2020 — With Lightweight Charts you have to implement any indicator you'd like to offer/use. Most if not all formula can be obtain online on ...
Lightweight Charts doesn't have the same concept of indicators as the other library. With Lightweight Charts you have to implement any indicator you'd like to offer/use.

name: Plugins | Lightweight Charts
snippet: Plugins allow you to extend the library's functionality and render custom elements, such as new series, drawing tools, indicators, and watermarks.

name: Analysis indicators | Lightweight Charts
snippet: Below is a list of indicators where each link points to their source code on GitHub. You can see all the indicators in action on the live demos page. Each indicator has two demos: Helper: shows the recommended method with automatic updates. Direct calculation: shows the method with a pure function.

name: lightweight-charts/indicator-examples at master - GitHub
snippet: This directory contains implementations of indicator calculations and examples of how they can be shown on a chart. To run these examples locally follow these steps: Visit localhost:5173 in the browser. Check the output in the compiled folder.
